content.problemStatement:
The **White Walkers** are coming.

The Winterfell is the first place to be attacked by the Night King and his army of the dead.

Jon Snow with Khaleesi has to take some important decisions to minimize the damage from the White Walkers.

Winterfell can be denoted as a graph with NNN nodes.

There are MMM roads each connecting 2 nodes - AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​.

The road is **directed from node** AiA_iAi​ to BiB_iBi​, **and not vice-versa**.

The power required to travel on this road is CiC_iCi​ **initially**.

Also, Winterfell is built in such a way that it is not possible to visit the same node twice using these roads. That is, you cannot start at a node, follow some of the roads and return back to the same node.

Jon has to stop the army of the dead.

All white walkers are standing at **node** 111 right now.

For this, he decided to carry out QQQ steps of two different types -


111   KKK

Add value KKK to the power required to travel each road in Winterfell. i.e after this operation Ci=Ci+KC_i = C_i + KCi​=Ci​+K  ∀ \forall∀ iii  ϵ[1,M]\epsilon [1,M]ϵ[1,M] . Value KKK can be negative as well.
222 YYY

Find the minimum power required for the white walkers to reach node YYY in Winterfell from node 111.
Help Jon is finding out the answer for each query of type 222.

###Input:


First line will contain 2 space separated integers - NNN and MMM, number of nodes , and the number of roads in Winterfell.
M lines follow. Each of the lines comprises 3 space separated integers  - AiA_iAi​, BiB_iBi​ and CiC_iCi​, denoting that ithi^{th}ith road is directed from node AiA_iAi​ to BiB_iBi​ with initial power required to travel this road being CiC_iCi​.
Next line comprises QQQ - the number of queries.
Q lines follow. Each query is of the type as described above, and will comprise 2 space separated integers. For type 111, the second integer will be KKK - the amount of power added to each road. For type 222, the second integer will be YYY - the node to reach from node 111.
###Output:
For each query of type 222, output in a single line answer to the minimum power required for white walkers at node 111 to reach node YYY.

If there is no way to reach node YYY, output **"No Path Exists" (without quotes)** .

###Constraints


1≤N≤30001 \leq N \leq 30001≤N≤3000
1≤M≤70001 \leq M \leq 70001≤M≤7000
1≤Q≤5∗1061 \leq Q \leq 5*10^61≤Q≤5∗106
1≤Ai,Bi≤N,Ai!=Bi1 \leq A_i, B_i \leq N, A_i != B_i1≤Ai​,Bi​≤N,Ai​!=Bi​
−109≤Ci,K≤109-10^9 \leq C_i, K \leq 10^9−109≤Ci​,K≤109
1≤Y≤N1 \leq Y \leq N1≤Y≤N
At any point of time, the power required to travel on each road will always be [−1012,1012][-10^{12}, 10^{12}][−1012,1012]
###Sample Input:

3 3

1 2 1

1 3 3

2 3 2

3

2 3

1 2

2 3

###Sample Output:

3

5

###EXPLANATION:
Initially, the road to reach node 333 with minimum power is either (1→2,2→3)( 1 \rightarrow 2, 2 \rightarrow 3)(1→2,2→3) or (1→3)( 1 \rightarrow 3 )(1→3), each amounting to total power = 333.

Then, the power requirement of each edge is increased by 222.

So, now the road (1→2,2→3)(1 \rightarrow 2, 2 \rightarrow  3)(1→2,2→3) requires total power = 3+4=73+4=73+4=7.

The road (1→3)(1 \rightarrow 3)(1→3) requires total power = 555.
